inline_review:
commit f1a3ace46c77c23b4e697d37370ff78f993b63ff
Author: Chenghao Duan <duanchenghao@kylinos.cn>

mm/memfd_luo: fix integer overflow in memfd_luo_preserve_folios

This commit changes the types of size and max_folios in
memfd_luo_preserve_folios to u64 to prevent integer truncation and
data loss when dealing with large sparse files.

Link: https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260323110747.193569-1-duanchenghao@kylinos.cn

> diff --git a/mm/memfd_luo.c b/mm/memfd_luo.c
> index fe8427c42d4e2..bed0f7d28677b 100644
> --- a/mm/memfd_luo.c
> +++ b/mm/memfd_luo.c
> @@ -90,8 +90,8 @@ static int memfd_luo_preserve_folios(struct file *file,
>  {
>  	struct inode *inode = file_inode(file);
>  	struct memfd_luo_folio_ser *folios_ser;
> -	unsigned int max_folios;
> -	long i, size, nr_pinned;
> +	u64 size, max_folios;
> +	long i, nr_pinned;

Does changing max_folios to u64 here fully resolve the integer truncation?

Looking at the call to memfd_pin_folios() later in this function:

        nr_pinned = memfd_pin_folios(file, 0, size - 1, folios, max_folios,
                                     &offset);

And the signature of memfd_pin_folios() in include/linux/mm.h:

long memfd_pin_folios(struct file *memfd, loff_t start, loff_t end,
                      struct folio **folios, unsigned int max_folios,
                      pgoff_t *offset);

Since memfd_pin_folios() still takes an unsigned int for max_folios, won't
the 64-bit max_folios value just be implicitly truncated to 32 bits when
passed as an argument? If max_folios was 0x100000001, it would still
truncate to 1 here, leaving the data loss issue unresolved.

Additionally, with max_folios now being a u64, the array allocation:

        folios = kvmalloc_objs(*folios, max_folios);

will attempt an allocation using the full 64-bit value. For a 16TB file
(0x100000001 folios), wouldn't this attempt a massive allocation (around
34GB) and risk an out-of-memory condition before the truncation even
happens?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Incomplete fix: `max_folios` integer truncation persists at the `memfd_pin_folios()` function call boundary, failing to prevent silent data loss while introducing a massive memory allocation risk.","severity":"Critical","severity_explanation":"The patch changes the local variable `max_folios` to `u64` to prevent overflow when calculating the required folios for sparse files larger than 16TB. However, it fails to update the external `memfd_pin_folios()` function (declared in `include/linux/mm.h`), which still expects `unsigned int max_folios`. When the 64-bit `max_folios` is passed to this function, the compiler implicitly truncates it down to 32 bits. For example, `0x100000001` folios will be truncated to `1`. `memfd_pin_folios()` will then pin only the first folio and the rest of the file's data will be silently lost across the live update, which means the patch entirely fails to fix the data loss bug it addresses. Furthermore, on 64-bit systems, the preceding `kvmalloc_objs(*folios, max_folios)` call (which accepts a 64-bit `size_t`) will use the full `u64` value to attempt a massive ~34 GB allocation for the pointer array, creating a severe OOM risk before the truncation even happens."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit f1a3ace46c77c23b4e697d37370ff78f993b63ff
Author: Chenghao Duan <duanchenghao@kylinos.cn>

mm/memfd_luo: fix integer overflow in memfd_luo_preserve_folios

This commit changes the types of size and max_folios in
memfd_luo_preserve_folios to u64 to prevent integer truncation and
data loss when dealing with large sparse files.

Link: https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260323110747.193569-1-duanchenghao@kylinos.cn

> diff --git a/mm/memfd_luo.c b/mm/memfd_luo.c
> index fe8427c42d4e2..bed0f7d28677b 100644
> --- a/mm/memfd_luo.c
> +++ b/mm/memfd_luo.c
> @@ -90,8 +90,8 @@ static int memfd_luo_preserve_folios(struct file *file,
>  {
>  	struct inode *inode = file_inode(file);
>  	struct memfd_luo_folio_ser *folios_ser;
> -	unsigned int max_folios;
> -	long i, size, nr_pinned;
> +	u64 size, max_folios;
> +	long i, nr_pinned;

Does changing max_folios to u64 here fully resolve the integer truncation?

Looking at the call to memfd_pin_folios() later in this function:

        nr_pinned = memfd_pin_folios(file, 0, size - 1, folios, max_folios,
                                     &offset);

And the signature of memfd_pin_folios() in include/linux/mm.h:

long memfd_pin_folios(struct file *memfd, loff_t start, loff_t end,
                      struct folio **folios, unsigned int max_folios,
                      pgoff_t *offset);

Since memfd_pin_folios() still takes an unsigned int for max_folios, won't
the 64-bit max_folios value just be implicitly truncated to 32 bits when
passed as an argument? If max_folios was 0x100000001, it would still
truncate to 1 here, leaving the data loss issue unresolved.

Additionally, with max_folios now being a u64, the array allocation:

        folios = kvmalloc_objs(*folios, max_folios);

will attempt an allocation using the full 64-bit value. For a 16TB file
(0x100000001 folios), wouldn't this attempt a massive allocation (around
34GB) and risk an out-of-memory condition before the truncation even
happens?

"}
